The Spokane baseball magnates
have decided where they want the
paE-ball park and If the objections
of some of the residents in that part
of the city can be overcome and the
city consents. the games for 1904
will be played on ground owned by
the city Of Spokane on the north side
Of the river. The location is on the
line Of the Spokane Traction company
and within a block Of the lines of the
Washington Water Power company
m Division street. 1t lies between
Mission and Sinto avenues and Nor
pnandie and Atlantic streets. Base
ball people consider 1t an admirable
location.

or the members Of the city council

with the light so far thrown on the
subject said they were opposed to 1t.
Some of the property holders, it is
Nsaid, will prepare protest against
hs location because Of the noise.
Especially on Sunday. and because
\they do not think 1t a help to their
property to have a baseball park in
their midst.


City would Get Little.


The Spokane Traction company last
night submitted to the city council
q petition for the rental of the prop
srty. which Is block 81 of Central ad-
dition. This Is q part of a tract
which the city secured a number OF
years ago In settlement of a claim
lars on deposit In the Browne Ma
tional bank when 1t failed. The COM
pany estimates the value of the block
at $4,000 and offers to pay All annual
rental of per cent of this valuation.
which would be 5000. But the COM
pany wants this rental to apply on
the cost Of grading the block which





proved to be the cost, the city would
receive it's first rental at the end Of
six and half years. The company
asks for 10-year lease, from which
the city would secure not to exceed
$3.50 under the proposition from the
company.


Suggests Several Conditions.


"Before would vote to grant this
lease and for 10 years." said Council
man Joseph b. Lindsey. Il must be
convinced that the city in that time
would not want the ground for the
storage of material. lumber or appar-
atus Of any kind which the city con-
tinually accumulates, that the prop
erty holders have no objection, that
the city will have no use for the
ground and that the company, will
assume all liability and taxes on the
property. 1 would not favor the city
granting the use of the park without
some adequate rental. which the COM
pany does not seem to have pro-
posed"


Hand Not In Favor.


IT am not in favor Of granting the
lease In its present FORM" said Coun-
Gilman Il. M. Hand. II do not care
to say at the present time what
changes l would ask"


II scarcely think I shall favor
granting the lease" said Councilman

against the use of this propertv for
